title: STUDY TOPIC Nativity part 10
devotional:
Luke 1:26-38

‘’In the sixth month of Elizabeth’s pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a village in Galilee, to a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of King David. Gabriel appeared to her and said, “Greetings, favoured woman! The Lord is with you! Confused and disturbed, Mary tried to think what the angel could mean. “Don’t be afraid, Mary,” the angel told her, “for you have found favour with God. You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus. He will be very great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his ancestor David. And he will reign over Israel forever; his Kingdom will never end!” Mary asked the angel, “But how can this happen? I am a virgin.” he angel replied, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the baby to be born will be holy, and he will be called the Son of God. What’s more, your relative Elizabeth has become pregnant in her old age! People used to say she was barren, but she has conceived a son and is now in her sixth month. For the word of God will never fail.” Mary responded, “I am the Lord’s servant. May everything you have said about me come true.” And then the angel left her.’’

You are totally accepted.

What I find fascinating about Mary is the thing that caused her to be confused and disturbed. Look at what the text says, ‘Gabriel appeared to her and said, “Greetings, favoured woman! The Lord is with you!. Confused and disturbed, Mary tried to think what the angel could mean.

The appearance of the angel Gabriel did not disturb her, but what he said about her. What is going on here? The English words “highly favoured” in the verse above are a translation of the Greek word charitoo. This word occurs only twice in the New Testament: Ephesians 1:6 and Luke 1:28. This word Charitoo is a verb, which in essence means to “bestow favour.” It indicates an action that God performed on those who have found favour in His sight. For Mary the thought that she was highly favoured by God was something she would have found difficult to accept, just like many of us.

We know in our heads that God loves and accepts us, but we live and think as if he doesn’t. This is why our insecurities have such a massive influence upon our lives, for we think God could not love us, or we need to do better or be better for God to love us. This ingrained belief, that we are not totally or really accepted by God, will rob us of the courage and resilience to do the will of God.

‘Mary, God the father has a wonderful plan for your life, but before you know that plan you need to know that you are completely loved and accepted by God.’

Read this out loud so you can hear it being spoken over you. ‘God’s love is so great for you that he wants to form Jesus in you.’ (See Galatians 4:19)
prayer: Father help me to accept your love so I can do your will.
html:
STUDY TOPIC Nativity part 10

Study Text:

Luke 1:26-38

‘’In the sixth month of Elizabeth’s pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a village in Galilee, to a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of King David. Gabriel appeared to her and said, “Greetings, favoured woman! The Lord is with you! Confused and disturbed, Mary tried to think what the angel could mean. “Don’t be afraid, Mary,” the angel told her, “for you have found favour with God. You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus. He will be very great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his ancestor David. And he will reign over Israel forever; his Kingdom will never end!” Mary asked the angel, “But how can this happen? I am a virgin.” he angel replied, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the baby to be born will be holy, and he will be called the Son of God. What’s more, your relative Elizabeth has become pregnant in her old age! People used to say she was barren, but she has conceived a son and is now in her sixth month. For the word of God will never fail.” Mary responded, “I am the Lord’s servant. May everything you have said about me come true.” And then the angel left her.’’

You are totally accepted.

What I find fascinating about Mary is the thing that caused her to be confused and disturbed. Look at what the text says, ‘Gabriel appeared to her and said, “Greetings, favoured woman! The Lord is with you!. Confused and disturbed, Mary tried to think what the angel could mean.

The appearance of the angel Gabriel did not disturb her, but what he said about her. What is going on here? The English words “highly favoured” in the verse above are a translation of the Greek word charitoo. This word occurs only twice in the New Testament: Ephesians 1:6 and Luke 1:28. This word Charitoo is a verb, which in essence means to “bestow favour.” It indicates an action that God performed on those who have found favour in His sight. For Mary the thought that she was highly favoured by God was something she would have found difficult to accept, just like many of us.

We know in our heads that God loves and accepts us, but we live and think as if he doesn’t. This is why our insecurities have such a massive influence upon our lives, for we think God could not love us, or we need to do better or be better for God to love us. This ingrained belief, that we are not totally or really accepted by God, will rob us of the courage and resilience to do the will of God.

‘Mary, God the father has a wonderful plan for your life, but before you know that plan you need to know that you are completely loved and accepted by God.’

Read this out loud so you can hear it being spoken over you. ‘God’s love is so great for you that he wants to form Jesus in you.’ (See Galatians 4:19)

Prayer: Father help me to accept your love so I can do your will.
Bible in a year reading plan: Nehemiah 4-6
